_README: Hand-labeled ground truth for the GKIN manipulation benchmark.
_instructions: 1. Add one object per labeled article. Required: article_id, ground_truth_fake.
2. Either set `text` directly OR set `url` (the runner will GET the URL and use the body).
3. ground_truth_fake: true if the article is factually fake/false. false if real.
4. ground_truth_manipulative: true if the article uses persuasion techniques
(loaded language, appeal to fear/anger, glittering generalities, etc.),
regardless of factual accuracy. A factually true article can still be manipulative.
5. Aim for >=30 entries with a roughly even real/fake split before running.
6. Delete this README block before the runner picks it up — or leave it; the runner
skips any entry without an `article_id` field.

title: Reuters wire example — REPLACE before running
text: PASTE THE FULL ARTICLE TEXT HERE (>=200 chars or the /analyze endpoint will reject it).
notes: Example template entry. Replace text + flags with real labeled content.
